brief: Plan a product detail page where shoppers need to inspect current product state, compare information, add the item to cart, and recover from cart feedback.

criteria.name: journey-clarity
expectedSignals: Product identity, price, and action appear before supporting details.
Comparison and related products come after primary inspection.

criteria.name: single-primary-action
expectedSignals: Add to cart is the clear primary task in the product summary.
Secondary exploration does not compete with the add-to-cart task.

criteria.name: proof-placement
expectedSignals: Price and product details are visible before related-product browsing.
Comparison support appears before users leave the product decision.

criteria.name: state-coverage
expectedSignals: Cart drawer has a closed state and focus behavior.
Cart toast covers feedback after add-to-cart.

criteria.name: register-fit
expectedSignals: The page prioritizes state, controls, comparison, feedback, and recovery.
Structure avoids brochure-style persuasion before the product task.

criteria.name: landmark-coverage
expectedSignals: Page includes header, main, and footer regions.
Store navigation and cart drawer include accessibility labels.

criteria.name: responsive-priority
expectedSignals: Mobile keeps media, summary, action, details, and related content in task order.
Tabs and related product areas stack without hiding the primary action.

criteria.name: anti-pattern-absence
expectedSignals: No brochure content appears before the product state and action.
No filler labels, missing responsive notes, or incomplete drawer contract.

notes: Positive fixture for utility/product quality. It complements schema and anti-pattern validators rather than replacing them.
